title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-26 · 委内瑞拉地震与国际救援
summary: 委内瑞拉地震死亡人数升至235人，国际救援物资开始抵达，但重型机械短缺。美国最高法院裁定边境官员可拒绝庇护申请，并允许终止海地、叙利亚等国的临时保护身份（TPS）。副总统万斯将水门事件比作深层政府阴谋。美国队在世界杯首场失利后仍晋级32强。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: It's still unclear whether anyone was hurt. The cause of the crash remains under investigation. And in Illinois, more than 30 -- 300 cannabis workers have gone on strike. Three hundred cannabis workers going on strike, alleging unfair labor practices. Their union accusing Ascend Wellness of not bargaining in good faith. All right. That does it for us here on CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 目前尚不清楚是否有人受伤。坠机原因仍在调查中。在伊利诺伊州，超过30 -- -- 300名大麻工人已举行罢工。300名大麻工人罢工，指控不公平的劳动行为。他们的工会指责Ascend Wellness没有真诚地进行谈判。好了，CNN头条快讯就到这里。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, Trump's mass deportation machine wins big at the Supreme Court. What the president might do with this increased power.
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中：特朗普的大规模驱逐机器在最高法院大获全胜。总统可能会利用这种增强的权力做什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Tessa Petit
paragraphs.en: My people, they can't go back. It's real. The reality in Haiti is real.
paragraphs.cn: 我的人民，他们回不去了。这是真的。海地的现实是真实的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And the desperate search through the rubble, the humanitarian struggle this morning, one day after a devastating earthquake in Venezuela. In the meantime, Vice President J.D. Vance compares himself to Richard Nixon and says Watergate was a deep-state conspiracy. What does he know that we don't? And Team USA, stunned by Turkey in the World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: 以及废墟中的绝望搜寻，今早的人道主义斗争，在委内瑞拉发生毁灭性地震一天后。与此同时，副总统J.D.万斯将自己比作理查德·尼克松，并称水门事件是深层政府的阴谋。他知道什么我们不知道的？美国队在世界杯上被土耳其震惊。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can they shake off the bad vibes and bounce back? And there continues the massive -- I want to bring you back to this massive search-and-rescue effort in Venezuela right now. The earthquake death toll continues to rise. Welcome back, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. This is where we begin. The death count at this point, 235. More than 4,300 people injured. And those figures are expected to rise as first responders sift through the rubble. Now, right now, this is before and after scene.
paragraphs.cn: 他们能否摆脱坏运气并反弹？而且大规模 -- -- 我想带您回到委内瑞拉目前的大规模搜救工作。地震死亡人数持续上升。欢迎回来，各位。我是奥蒂·科尼什。我们开始。目前死亡人数为235人。超过4300人受伤。随着急救人员在废墟中搜寻，这些数字预计还会上升。现在，这是震前和震后的场景。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, you can get a sense of what the quakes took out. The first 48 to 72 hours are widely regarded as the golden window to reach people who may still be buried alive. So, this is new video showing passengers panicking aboard a plane at the international airport North of Caracas as the powerful tremors shook the aircraft on the tarmac. CNN's Stefano Pozzebon joins us now from Bogota, Colombia. We spoke yesterday, and I was asking about regional aid.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，您可以了解地震造成了什么破坏。最初的48到72小时被广泛认为是接触可能仍被活埋者的黄金窗口。这是新视频，显示加拉加斯北部国际机场一架飞机上的乘客惊慌失措，因为强烈震动使停机坪上的飞机摇晃。CNN的斯特凡诺·波佐邦现在从哥伦比亚波哥大加入我们。我们昨天谈过，我问到了地区援助。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can you talk about now? What's happening as more and more people come in to try and support the rescue effort?
paragraphs.cn: 您现在能谈谈吗？随着越来越多的人前来支持救援工作，情况如何？

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.en: Yes, Audie. I think that that is a smart point, a smart angle to explore today, is that today is the day we are finally seeing the aid coming in. The Dominicans arrived yesterday at around 9 p.m. local time in Venezuela. I believe that they were the first mission of international aid relief landing in Caracas. Those are definitely the first images that we had seen here at CNN overnight. The Colombians were also able to fly into Caracas with two Hercules C- 130.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，奥蒂。我认为今天探索这个角度很明智，今天我们终于看到援助到来。多米尼加人昨天当地时间晚上9点左右抵达委内瑞拉。我相信他们是第一批降落在加拉加斯的国际救援队。这绝对是我们在CNN整夜看到的第一批图像。哥伦比亚人也能够用两架C-130大力神运输机飞入加拉加斯。

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.en: That, of course, took time, because the runway itself at the international airport, which serves the city of Caracas, was badly damaged. You can see from that video that you just played that the quake hit with the plane on the tarmac and created open cracks, really, on the tarmac of the runway.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，这花了时间，因为服务于加拉加斯市的国际机场跑道本身严重受损。从您刚才播放的视频中可以看到，地震发生时飞机在停机坪上，并在跑道停机坪上造成了裂缝。

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.en: So, these military planes that carry the aid had to make some adjustments in order to arrive as quickly and as close as possible to the relief zone, which is the state of La Guaira, where the airport is located, which is this coastal state close to Caracas, but on the coast on the Caribbean coast, where the majority of the damage is -- is located and being confined. So, today's the day we're going to see more international aid pouring in. The United States have also arrived on the scene overnight.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，这些运送援助的军用飞机不得不进行调整，以便尽可能快速、尽可能接近救援区抵达，救援区是拉瓜伊拉州，机场所在地位于该州，这是一个靠近加拉加斯的沿海州，但在加勒比海岸，大部分损害发生并被限制在那里。所以，今天我们将看到更多国际援助涌入。美国也在夜间抵达现场。

paragraphs.speaker: Stefano Pozzebon
paragraphs.en: A major general from the Marine Corps arrived in Caracas. By the way, it's still surreal to me that we are seeing a major marine general in the grounds of Caracas less than six months after the ousting of Maduro. But this is the time we're living and were living in. And -- and this general arrived to coordinate efforts from the United States and other countries to try and help the Venezuelan population -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 一名海军陆战队少将抵达加拉加斯。顺便说一句，在马杜罗被推翻不到六个月后，我们看到一名海军陆战队少将出现在加拉加斯，这对我来说仍然很不真实。但这就是我们生活的时代。这位将军前来协调美国和其他国家的努力，试图帮助委内瑞拉人民 -- -- 奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Stefano, I don't know if you've been able to hear from the region directly, but if you are, what's the sense of what's needed now beyond this search-and-rescue moment? I think heavy machinery is the trickiest thing, because one thing is to bring in containers with medicines, with plasters, with emergency kit, with generators.
paragraphs.cn: 斯特凡诺，我不知道你是否能直接从该地区听到消息，但如果你能，除了当前的搜救时刻，你认为现在还需要什么？我认为重型机械是最棘手的问题，因为一方面可以运来装有药品、石膏、急救包和发电机的集装箱。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But what do they need in La Guaira is excavator; big machinery in order to lift in a safely way, the -- all of these floors that have been pancaked on top of each other because of the -- of the power of the tremors. And Venezuela don't have those machinery readily available. We've seen some of them in action in Caracas in the last couple of days. Our team is at the city, and they have been filming it.
paragraphs.cn: 但拉瓜伊拉需要的是挖掘机；大型机械，以便安全地抬起那些因地震威力而层层叠叠的楼板。而委内瑞拉没有现成的这些机械。过去几天我们在加拉加斯看到了一些机械在运作。我们的团队就在该市，他们一直在拍摄。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But in La Guaira yesterday, when they were down in the area most affected, they were really talking about the lack of equipment, the lack of gear. So, I think that that is a crucial equipment, a crucial component that the country desperately needs as quickly as possible.
paragraphs.cn: 但昨天在拉瓜伊拉，当他们到达受灾最严重的地区时，他们确实在谈论缺乏设备和装备。所以，我认为这是该国迫切需要的关键设备和关键组成部分，越快越好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Stefano Pozzebon, speaking to us from neighboring Colombia, thank you so much. Now, while the people in Venezuela are desperate for help, we here in the U.S. have seen two rulings from the Supreme Court indicating that the U.S. is not quite the place of refuge it might have been. The Supreme Court ruled immigration officials at the U.S.-Mexico border can turn away asylum seekers before they enter the country.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。斯特凡诺·波泽邦，从邻国哥伦比亚为我们报道，非常感谢。现在，当委内瑞拉人民急需帮助时，我们在美国看到了最高法院的两项裁决，表明美国可能不再是曾经的避难所。最高法院裁定，美国-墨西哥边境的移民官员可以在寻求庇护者进入美国之前将其拒之门外。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, in her dissent, Justice Sonia Sotomayor wrote, "The consequences of today's decision are predictable. More people will die." Now, the justices also allowed the Trump administration to strip temporary protected status for hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians living in the
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在她的反对意见中，索尼娅·索托马约尔大法官写道："今天裁决的后果是可以预见的。更多人将会死亡。"此外，大法官们还允许特朗普政府取消对数十万在美国的海地人和叙利亚人的临时保护身份。

paragraphs.speaker: U.S. Tom Homan
paragraphs.en: The whole statute exists. Temporarily to give people protection while the country's in turmoil or after they suffer a hurricane. But the problem is, no administration has had the guts to actually follow that statute. President Trump has the guts to follow the law. So temporary means temporary. When the condition in that country gets better, they need to go home. So temporary means temporary. And I'm grateful for that decision.
paragraphs.cn: 整个法规都存在。临时保护身份是为了在国家动荡或遭受飓风后暂时给予人们保护。但问题是，没有哪届政府有勇气真正遵循该法规。特朗普总统有勇气遵守法律。所以临时就意味着临时。当该国的状况好转时，他们需要回家。所以临时就是临时。我对这一决定表示感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, TPS protects people who are fleeing violence and natural disasters.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，TPS保护的是逃离暴力和自然灾害的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Mercedes Narcisse
paragraphs.en: This same government that tells me that it is too dangerous to set foot in Haiti, just told 350,000 Haitians that they have to go back there anyway, even to their death sentence. You cannot have it both ways. This is not Burger King.
paragraphs.cn: 同一个政府告诉我，踏上海地太危险，却刚刚告诉35万海地人，他们无论如何都必须回去，即使是去送死。你不能两全其美。这不是汉堡王。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, today in the group chat, we've got our CNN legal analyst, former federal prosecutor Elliot Williams; also Rob Bluey, president and executive editor at "The Daily Signal"; and Lulu Garcia-Navarro, CNN contributor and host of "The New York Times" podcast, "The Interview." Thank you so much for being here. Elliot, I've got to start with you, the legal of this. Basically, this law from the 1990s gives people a kind of quasi legal status. You can work a bit, you can stay a bit, but it is by definition limited --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，今天在我们的群聊中，我们有CNN法律分析师、前联邦检察官埃利奥特·威廉姆斯；还有《每日信号》的总裁兼执行主编罗布·布鲁伊；以及CNN撰稿人、《纽约时报》播客《访谈》的主持人露露·加西亚-纳瓦罗。非常感谢你们的到来。埃利奥特，我得从你开始，谈谈法律方面的问题。基本上，这项1990年代的法律赋予人们一种准合法身份。你可以工作一点，可以待一段时间，但根据定义，它是有限的 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- unless it gets continual extensions.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 除非它不断获得延期。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, does Homan have a point? And is that what the court underscored, or did they point to something else?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，霍曼说得有道理吗？这是法院强调的重点，还是他们指出了其他问题？

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: They pointed to something else. What he does have a point in that -- so the point of temporary protected status is when conditions in a country are such that people cannot return there. Haiti was an excellent example after the earthquake. Certainly, it was not feasible for the United States to send people back there. It was safe --
paragraphs.cn: 他们指出了其他问题。他说的有道理的地方在于 -- -- 临时保护身份的意义在于，当一个国家的状况使得人们无法返回时，美国可以给予保护。海地在地震后就是一个很好的例子。显然，美国当时不可能把人们送回那里。那里是安全的 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Or Syria with the war.
paragraphs.cn: 或者叙利亚，因为战争。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: With the war and the deteriorating conditions there. Now, what countless administrations of both parties have done is extend those designations each time and sometimes multiple times, up to a period of years. Now, on the flip side, what Kristi Noem, the former secretary of homeland security, did was simply just suspend TPS for Haitians and Syrians.
paragraphs.cn: 因为战争和不断恶化的局势。现在，两党历届政府所做的就是每次延长这些指定，有时甚至多次延长，长达数年之久。另一方面，前国土安全部部长克里斯蒂·诺姆所做的就是直接暂停海地和叙利亚人的TPS。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And I'm going to put up a map showing all the countries --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我将展示一张地图，上面标明了所有国家 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- the administration has tried to terminate --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 本届政府试图终止 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- TPS. Just so people have a sense of where folks are coming from and where we're trying to stop them from.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- TPS。只是为了让人们了解这些人的来源地以及我们试图阻止他们从哪里来。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: And -- but here, there wasn't a ton of process. It was just simply, with the stroke of a pen, we're going -- we' re going to suspend this right here. That was part of what animated the lawsuit. But certainly, you know, again, Tom Homan, frankly, who I worked with for years at ICE and whom I know, he's got a little bit of a point in that the sort of the temporary nature of all of this.
paragraphs.cn: 而且 -- -- 但在这里，并没有太多的程序。只是简单地大笔一挥，我们就 -- -- 我们就暂停了这项权利。这是引发诉讼的部分原因。但当然，你知道，坦率地说，汤姆·霍曼，我在ICE与他共事多年，我了解他，他说的有一点道理，就是这一切的临时性质。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: That said, the manner in which they did it, and the potential consequences, in light of the Venezuelan earthquake, could be profound for the entire hemisphere. And it's just something to think about what comes next after this Supreme Court decision, like any other.
paragraphs.cn: 尽管如此，他们采取的方式以及潜在的后果，考虑到委内瑞拉的地震，可能对整个半球产生深远影响。就像其他任何最高法院裁决一样，接下来会发生什么，值得思考。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There was a twist in this conversation, which is that the Haitian plaintiffs said, Look, this administration has said such heinous things about Haitians in particular that there's obviously racial animus in the way they made this decision about TPS for their status. And it was interesting.
paragraphs.cn: 这场对话中有一个转折点，海地原告表示："看，本届政府针对海地人说了如此恶毒的话，显然他们在决定海地人的临时保护身份时带有种族敌意。"这很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You had Elena Kagan writing, "The statements fairly shout, in their racial undertones and overtones alike, that race entered into the president's resolve to remove Haitians from this country." I'm not sure if we have some clips of that. We might, but needless to say, we have been hearing over the years, especially during campaign time, when Vance accused Haitians of all manner of thing, and that the court rejected that.
paragraphs.cn: 埃琳娜·卡根写道："这些言论在种族潜台词和明示中都大声宣告，种族因素进入了总统将海地人驱逐出这个国家的决心。"我不确定我们是否有相关片段。可能有，但不用说，多年来我们一直听到，尤其是在竞选期间，万斯指控海地人各种事情，而法院驳回了这些指控。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: ROB BLUEY, PRESIDENT/EXECUTIVE EDITOR, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Well, Audie, I'm glad you brought up the campaign, because I think going back to this conversation, just more broadly on immigration, Donald Trump promised to carry out the largest mass deportation in our country's history. The fact of the matter is, if you look at the first year of the Trump administration, they did not hit the mark on the president's own promise.
paragraphs.cn: 《每日信号》总裁兼执行编辑罗布·布鲁伊：嗯，奥黛，我很高兴你提到竞选，因为我认为回到这场对话，更广泛地谈论移民问题，唐纳德·特朗普承诺进行我国历史上最大规模的驱逐出境。事实是，如果你看特朗普政府的第一年，他们并没有实现总统自己的承诺。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And now, the mass deportation coalition is saying that they need to deport up to a million illegal immigrants this year.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，大规模驱逐联盟表示，他们今年需要驱逐多达100万非法移民。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, you're bringing in -- are you bringing this up because you're saying this was a win they needed?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，你提出这个是因为你认为这是他们需要的一场胜利吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Yes. I'm saying that Donald Trump is -- if that's the promise that he was going to deliver on -- and that was a central theme of his 2024 campaign -- these victories at the Supreme Court certainly put him on the pathway to getting back on track when it comes to carrying out that. Now, we may disagree on whether or not that's the policy the United States should carry out. I just happen to say, as a conservative and as somebody who --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我是说唐纳德·特朗普 -- -- 如果那是他要兑现的承诺 -- -- 并且那是他2024年竞选的核心主题 -- -- 那么最高法院的这些胜利肯定让他走上了实现这一目标的轨道。现在，我们可能对这是否是美国应该执行的政策有分歧。我只是碰巧认为，作为一个保守派和作为一个人 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You're happy about this --
paragraphs.cn: 你对此感到高兴 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: I think that these rogue judges were -- were out of step. Absolutely I do.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这些无赖法官是 -- -- 是脱离现实的。我绝对这么认为。

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Well, the first thing is these were not illegal immigrants. These were people who were here and were given legal status. They did all the things that they needed to do. Specifically, they got vetted. They have jobs. They are nurses. They are doctors. They are journalists.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，首先这些人不是非法移民。他们是合法身份在这里的人。他们做了所有需要做的事情。具体来说，他们经过了审查。他们有工作。他们是护士、医生、记者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm going to put up, while you're talking, an example of the kinds of jobs Haitian TPS holders were doing in this country, 200,000 of them in the U.S. workforce.
paragraphs.cn: 在你说话的时候，我举一个例子，说明海地临时保护身份持有者在这个国家从事的工作类型，其中有20万人在美国劳动力队伍中。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Exactly. And so, the idea that -- the problem that I have always with this argument is -- made by conservatives is that they conflate everything, right? It's like, illegal immigrants, TPS holders. It's basically anyone who's brown who has been allowed in this country. We have seen under this administration, basically, refugees, the entire refugee program being suspended and being now handed over to white South Africans.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。所以，我一直对这个论点有意见 -- -- 保守派提出的 -- -- 就是他们把一切都混为一谈，对吧？比如，非法移民、TPS持有者。基本上就是所有被允许进入这个国家的棕色人种。我们看到在这届政府下，基本上，难民，整个难民项目被暂停，现在又交给了南非白人。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: What is this about, if not about racial animus? And I think what Elena Kagan was pointing to were the president's very own statements. "They're eating the dogs. They're eating the cats," talking about Haitians who were working. And that was completely fabricated. And so, I think at the end of the day, when you look specifically at a place like Haiti, how can you argue that that is safe for people to go back to?
paragraphs.cn: 如果不是出于种族敌意，那这又是怎么回事？我认为埃琳娜·卡根所指出的正是总统自己的言论。"他们在吃狗，他们在吃猫，"说的是那些工作的海地人。那完全是捏造的。所以，我认为归根结底，当你具体看海地这样的地方时，你怎么能说那里安全到可以让人回去呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But I hear your point that, again, conflating mass deportation of illegal immigrants --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但我听到你的观点，再次强调，将大规模驱逐非法移民混为一谈 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- is that the same as haphazard deportation of legal migrants that you have approved and vetted? And why lump those two together?
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 这与随意驱逐你批准并审查过的合法移民是一回事吗？为什么要把这两者混为一谈？

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Well, I do think that we have a massive problem. What, upwards of 20 million people who entered the country illegally under Joe Biden's watch. So, there's --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我确实认为我们有一个大问题。在拜登的监管下，有超过2000万人非法入境。所以，这 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, no. I want to repeat --
paragraphs.cn: 哦，不。我想重复 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: -- there's that. There's that -- that -- there's that issue.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 有这个问题。有那个 -- -- 那个 -- -- 有那个问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- the legal migrants. Why put those together? I just want to make sure you get a chance to answer that point.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 合法移民。为什么要把它们放在一起？我只是想确保你有机会回答那个问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Yes. Well, if we're going to go back to the Supreme Court decision, ultimately, it comes down to the fact that it's up to the secretary of homeland security to make this decision. And it's not a matter for the courts to review. That's ultimately what --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好吧，如果我们回到最高法院的裁决，最终归结为一点，即由国土安全部部长来做这个决定。这不是法院可以审查的事情。最终这就是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, I think they said it was Congress. But let me just add one more thing.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为他们说是国会。但让我再补充一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Congress gave the authority --
paragraphs.cn: 国会将这一权力授予了 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: -- to the secretary of homeland security.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 国土安全部部长。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me follow Lulu's point.
paragraphs.cn: 让我顺着卢卢的观点说。

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: And these rogue judges were interfering in that.
paragraphs.cn: 而那些无赖法官正在干涉这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because that's not what they have in their decision here. But, Lulu, I do want to answer something you said about the racial animus. Alito spoke to this directly, and he said, Look, political discourse by prominent public figures is increasingly couched in terms that would have scandalized the public just a short time ago. And he talks about Haitian -- the commentary around Haitians in particular.
paragraphs.cn: 因为他们的裁决中并没有体现这一点。但是，卢卢，我确实想回应你提到的关于种族敌意的问题。阿利托直接谈到了这一点，他说，看，知名公众人物的政治言论越来越多地使用那些不久前还会让公众感到震惊的措辞。他特别提到了关于海地人的评论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But he says, "Whatever one may think of the kinds of statements that the -- the state's lawyers couldn't even repeat in court," he says, "they're insufficient to show that the termination of Haiti's TPS designation was based on the race of the Haitian people." So, you could talk about eating the dogs. You can say all kinds of stuff. And they're saying that doesn't necessarily mean that that is what is driving the decision. That it might be the things that he's talking about and that that is across the board.
paragraphs.cn: 但他说："无论人们如何看待这些 -- -- 州律师甚至无法在法庭上重复的言论，"他说，"它们都不足以证明终止海地TPS身份是基于海地人的种族。"所以，你可以谈论吃狗肉。你可以说各种东西。而他们认为这并不一定意味着这就是推动决策的原因。可能只是他谈论的事情，而且这是普遍存在的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Well, because there -- no one could establish definitively in court that we are removing Haitian people because they're Haitian, legally, there -- Justice Alito is making the point that, of course, there's no tie between the two. But -- but, you know, to Lulu's point, look at the president's own statements, literally calling Haiti a shithole country. You cannot -- I think we're being a little bit cute if we look the other way when there is an immigration policy that --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，因为 -- -- 没有人能在法庭上明确证明我们驱逐海地人是因为他们是海地人，在法律上，阿利托大法官指出，两者之间当然没有联系。但是 -- -- 但是，你知道，针对卢卢的观点，看看总统自己的言论，他简直把海地称为一个粪坑国家。你不能 -- -- 我认为，当存在一项移民政策时，我们如果视而不见，那就有点太天真了 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But this approach on race is consistent for this court.
paragraphs.cn: 但法院在种族问题上的这种做法是一贯的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Yes. Oh, absolutely.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。哦，绝对如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. OK. We are going to come back to some of these issues, but stay with us. Because from Sunday school to public school, there is this big debate in Texas over a move to bring Bible stories into the classroom. We're going to talk about that. And then that top FEMA official who once claimed that he teleported to a Waffle House, is now out of a job. And World Cup stunner. Team USA fans not fazed at all after the men's national team was upset in the final minute against Turkey.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好的。我们稍后会回到其中一些问题，但请继续关注我们。因为从主日学校到公立学校，德克萨斯州正在就一项将圣经故事引入课堂的举措展开激烈辩论。我们将讨论这个问题。还有那位曾声称自己瞬移到华夫饼店的FEMA高级官员，现在被解雇了。以及世界杯的冷门。美国男足在最后一分钟被土耳其队逆转，但球迷们毫不在意。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: It's weird to think that we lost, but it didn't really mean anything, and we're still going through first place in our bracket. So, I mean, this experience was phenomenal.
paragraphs.cn: 想到我们输了，感觉很奇怪，但这其实没什么大不了的，我们仍然在小组中排名第一。所以，我的意思是，这次经历太棒了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Its now 18 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. Luigi Mangione's attorneys discuss a possible plea deal with prosecutors in his federal case, but sources say the discussions didn't result into an agreement. Mangione is due back in federal court on Monday. And later this morning, the Texas Board of Education will vote on a proposal about adding Bible stories as part of public-school curriculum. Now, it would be required reading for students from elementary all the way through high school.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是整点过18分。这是你的早间新闻汇总。路易吉·曼吉奥内的律师正在与联邦检察官讨论可能的认罪协议，但消息人士称讨论未能达成协议。曼吉奥内将于周一再次出庭联邦法院。今天上午晚些时候，德克萨斯州教育委员会将就一项将圣经故事纳入公立学校课程的提案进行投票。如果通过，从小学到高中，学生都将被要求阅读这些内容。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Supporters call it essential context. Critics warn it blurs the line between church and state. And one of FEMA's most consequential leadership roles is still empty. The man who made this claim is out.
paragraphs.cn: 支持者称其为必要的背景知识。批评者警告说，这模糊了政教分离的界限。而联邦紧急事务管理局（FEMA）最重要的领导职位之一仍然空缺。提出这一说法的人已经离职。

paragraphs.speaker: Gregg Phillips
paragraphs.en: We had a teleport incident, two of them. The car lifted up and took me where I was going, and I ended up at a Waffle House, like 50 miles away from where I was.
paragraphs.cn: 我们发生了一起传送事件，实际上是两起。汽车升起来，把我带到了我要去的地方，最后我到了一家华夫饼屋，离我原来所在的地方大约50英里远。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, this came up, saying he teleported to a Waffle House. And guess what? He's out at FEMA. Several sources tell CNN it was not voluntary. DHS saying Gregg Phillips' departure was for personal reasons. And later on CNN THIS MORNING, disaster in Venezuela. Survivors face a humanitarian fight and new hurdles if they try to seek asylum in the U.S. Plus, Team USA's first stumble in the World Cup group stage. They begin play in the knockout round next week. In the meantime, good morning to Chicago.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以，这件事被提了出来，说他传送到了华夫饼屋。你猜怎么着？他离开了FEMA。多位消息人士告诉CNN，这不是自愿的。国土安全部表示，格雷格·菲利普斯的离职是出于个人原因。稍后在CNN《今晨》节目中：委内瑞拉的灾难。幸存者面临人道主义斗争，以及如果他们试图在美国寻求庇护将遇到的新障碍。此外，美国队在世界杯小组赛中的首次失利。他们将于下周开始淘汰赛阶段的比赛。与此同时，芝加哥早上好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, in the World Cup. Team USA has suffered a stunning 3-2 loss to Turkey, because it happened in the final seconds of the match, Turkey snapping a 2-2 tie in the 98th minute to capture their first win of the tournament. Now for the U.S., it was their first loss. The Americans are already advancing onto the next round, and they face Bosnia and Herzegovina next week in San Francisco. Now, Turkey was going home regardless. But was this a blow to the confidence of the men's team?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，在世界杯上。美国队以2比3惨败给土耳其，因为比赛在最后几秒发生了转折，土耳其在第98分钟打破2比2平局，赢得了他们在本届赛事中的首场胜利。对美国队来说，这是他们的首场失利。美国人已经晋级下一轮，他们将于下周在旧金山对阵波黑。而土耳其无论结果如何都将回家。但这是否打击了男队的信心？

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian Berhalter
paragraphs.en: I think you saw the second half, how we came out. And, you know, I think they deserve more like one moment. You know, we slipped in the last second of the game. And you know, I think we're proud of the goals put in. You know I think the guys, you know, did well. And you know, we fought. And you know, unfortunate not to get the result. But we'll be ready for sure.
paragraphs.cn: 我想你看到了下半场我们是如何出场的。而且，你知道，我认为他们更值得一个时刻。你知道，我们在比赛最后一秒失误了。而且，你知道，我们为打入的进球感到自豪。你知道，我认为队员们做得很好。而且，你知道，我们战斗了。你知道，不幸的是没有取得结果。但我们肯定会准备好的。

paragraphs.speaker: Brenden Aaronson
paragraphs.en: You can always take these things as fuel. You know, having that moment, you know, in the last one where they score, it's -- it's tough. You know, we wanted to walk away with, you know, no loss in the group stage. But, you know, we've got to take it as it was still a fantastic group stage.
paragraphs.cn: 你总是可以把这些当作动力。你知道，在最后一刻他们进球的那一刻，这很艰难。你知道，我们本想以小组赛不败的战绩离开。但是，你知道，我们必须接受它，这仍然是一个精彩的小组赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, Will Leitch is here to help me translate. He's a contributing editor at "New York Magazine," columnist with "The Washington Post." You are my World Cup whisperer. And thanks to the -- the Knicks, I'm -- I'm scared of, like, momentum killers. Do you know what I mean? Like, I'm unnecessarily freaked out, but no one else is, I've noticed. Why is that? WILL LEITCH, COLUMNIST, "WASHINGTON POST": Yes, certainly. Coach Mauricio Pochettino, who's Argentinian, he talked to a lot of reporters after the game.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，威尔·莱奇在这里帮我翻译。他是《纽约杂志》的特约编辑，《华盛顿邮报》的专栏作家。你是我的世界杯解说员。感谢尼克斯队，我害怕那种扼杀势头的东西。你明白我的意思吗？比如，我莫名其妙地感到恐慌，但我注意到其他人并没有。这是为什么？《华盛顿邮报》专栏作家威尔·莱奇：是的，当然。阿根廷籍教练毛里西奥·波切蒂诺在赛后接受了许多记者采访。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He was very confused, because he was, like, why are you so negative? We won the group. And I'm like, well, you're welcome to a dealing with the American media. But yes, at a certain level he is -- he's right. The U.S. won their group. They actually played almost none of their starters last night, not only to rest them, but also strategic reason. Remember, a lot of those players had yellow cards.
paragraphs.cn: 他非常困惑，因为他觉得，你们为什么这么消极？我们赢得了小组赛。而我想，好吧，欢迎你体验与美国媒体打交道。但确实，在某种程度上他是对的。美国队赢得了小组赛。他们昨晚实际上几乎没有派上任何首发球员，不仅是为了让他们休息，也是出于战略原因。记住，很多球员都有黄牌在身。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Those yellow cards reset -- reset after this round, which means they're clear to play and don't have to worry about missing the next round. The goal of this was to keep everyone healthy to -- to, to make sure that Pulisic was able to come in and play and be a little healthy. He showed that he was fine. The result, certainly, they would have liked to have won, I suppose, but it really didn't make any difference. Turkey didn't have anything to play either -- play for either. The U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 那些黄牌清零 -- -- 在本轮比赛后清零，这意味着他们可以安心上场，不必担心错过下一轮。这样做的目的是让每个人都保持健康，确保普利西奇能够上场并保持一定的健康状态。他证明了自己状态良好。至于结果，他们当然希望赢，但我想这其实无关紧要。土耳其队也没什么可争的。美国队

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: was set and locked in to play in their game on Wednesday, no matter what happened in this game. And the coach kind of sort of treated it that way.
paragraphs.cn: 已经确定要在周三的比赛中出场，无论本场比赛结果如何。而教练也基本上是这样处理的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, here's a fan with their assessment, which I found super interesting. So, here it is.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，这里有一位球迷的评价，我觉得非常有趣。那么，来看看吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Again, I think today, they showed that even their second string could come in and compete against a world-class team. We're so excited for the next round. I think we're going to beat maybe Bosnia-Herzegovina. We're going to play hard. We're going to crush them. It's going to be awesome.
paragraphs.cn: 再说一次，我认为今天他们展示了即使是替补阵容也能与世界级球队抗衡。我们对下一轮比赛非常兴奋。我觉得我们可能会击败波黑。我们会全力以赴。我们会碾压他们。那将非常棒。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: Awesome. Thank you so much.
paragraphs.cn: 太棒了。非常感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's the first part of that. Even their second team can compete against a world-class team. I feel like we should underscore that, given just how advanced the women's game on the U.S. side has been for so long. But people haven't talked about men the same way. So, help me parse what this fan is saying.
paragraphs.cn: 这是其中的第一部分。即使是他们的替补阵容也能与世界级球队抗衡。我觉得我们应该强调这一点，因为美国女足长期以来一直处于领先地位。但人们并没有以同样的方式谈论男足。所以，帮我分析一下这位球迷在说什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Yes, there really was. And listen, you know, think of like maybe Tyler Kolek, to use our Knicks example, playing a lot and being kind of hold-up. This speaks so much to how much progress the U.S. national team has made. Again, they're clearly not as good as the women's team, but realize for years and years and years in the World Cup, the U.S. has played a very defensive game. They've just tried to kind of not lose. That's not -- they've been very aggressive this entire time. They were aggressive last night.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，确实如此。听着，你知道，想想比如泰勒·科莱克，用我们尼克斯队的例子，他上场时间很多，但有点拖沓。这充分说明了美国国家队取得了多大的进步。再说一次，他们显然不如女足，但要知道，在世界杯上，美国队多年来一直踢得非常保守。他们只是试图不输球。但这次他们一直非常积极。昨晚他们就很积极。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Frankly, they were actually the better team last night. The result didn't show that. But generally speaking, they were better pretty much across the board. Pulisic, again, looked so good in his short time in. These are all the backups, and I think those -- they're going to be fully rested for the game on Wednesday. That's the idea. There was a time where the starters for the U.S. national team could not compete on the national stage.
paragraphs.cn: 坦率地说，昨晚他们实际上是表现更好的球队。结果并没有体现这一点。但总的来说，他们在几乎所有方面都更胜一筹。普利西奇在短暂的上场时间里再次表现出色。这些都是替补球员，我认为他们将在周三的比赛中得到充分休息。这就是目的。曾几何时，美国国家队的首发球员都无法在国际舞台上竞争。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Now, the backups not only were able to compete, they really were the better team for most of the team [SIC] last night in a game that, again, did not matter and was treated that way by everyone. Most of all, the coach.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，替补球员不仅能够竞争，而且在昨晚那场无关紧要、所有人都如此对待的比赛中，他们在大部分时间里实际上是表现更好的球队。尤其是教练。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And one other thing. Will. You and I talked last year about whether the U.S. would become a kind of sports villain, right? With its threats against Greenland, starting up things against Venezuela or, you know, with Iran. That -- I think we were wrong. That's not turned out to be the case. It's been kind of a love fest.
paragraphs.cn: 还有一件事。威尔，去年我们讨论过美国是否会成为某种体育界的反派，对吧？因为其对格陵兰的威胁、对委内瑞拉或伊朗的挑衅。我想我们错了。事实并非如此。反而成了一场爱的盛宴。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: It really has. You know, a lot of the worries that people had heading into this World Cup -- from, like, ICE showing up at events or -- or even Trump kind of going with the Knicks idea, showing up and kind of distracting. None of that's happened yet. Now we are not even to the knockout stage yet. But certainly, you know, one of the fun things about the U.S. men's national team, they are something that Americans generally think of themselves as being, but aren't: underdogs. Like, they are kind of a growth stock.
paragraphs.cn: 确实如此。你知道，人们在本届世界杯前有很多担忧 -- -- 比如移民执法局出现在赛事现场，或者特朗普突然提出尼克斯队的想法，现身并分散注意力。这些都没有发生。现在我们甚至还没到淘汰赛阶段。但可以肯定的是，美国男足国家队的一个有趣之处在于，他们代表了美国人通常自认为但实际并非如此的形象：弱势方。他们有点像成长型股票。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: They're a fun team to get excited. Because while they can compete on the world stage, they're not a favorite like a Spain or a France or an England. But they are good, and they are getting better. Those are fun teams to root for. We always think of ourselves as Rocky Balboa. var CNN = CNN || {}; WILL LEITCH, CONTRIBUTING EDITOR, "NEW YORK MAGAZINE": They can't compete on the world stage if they are not a favorite like a Spain or a France or an England, but they are good and they are getting better.
paragraphs.cn: 他们是一支令人兴奋的球队。因为虽然他们能在世界舞台上竞争，但他们不像西班牙、法国或英格兰那样是热门球队。但他们很优秀，而且正在变得更好。这样的球队值得支持。我们总把自己想象成洛奇·巴尔博亚。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Those are fun teams to root for. We always think of ourselves as Rocky Balboa, but usually we're not.
paragraphs.cn: 这样的球队值得支持。我们总把自己想象成洛奇·巴尔博亚，但通常我们不是。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: In the closest there we are. Like, this is the closest you kind of get for that. We are this -- the closest as a U.S. national team will ever get to being Hoosiers. So, that's a fun team to kind of get excited and cheer.
paragraphs.cn: 这次最接近了。这是美国国家队最接近成为《火爆教头》中球队的一次。所以，这是一支令人兴奋和值得欢呼的球队。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Good reference. Will Leitch, thank you so much.
paragraphs.cn: 好比喻。威尔·莱奇，非常感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to follow up on what's going on with the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. accusing Iran of attacking a vessel and pausing U.N. efforts to evacuate ships. Plus, why did J.D. Vance downplay the impact of Watergate?
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，我们将跟进霍尔木兹海峡的局势。美国指责伊朗袭击了一艘船只，并暂停了联合国疏散船只的努力。此外，J.D.万斯为何淡化水门事件的影响？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Developing this morning, a ship has struck the Strait of Hormuz. A ship has been struck in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. is pointing the finger at Iran, saying one of their drones hit the cargo ship. It's the first strike since the U.S. and Iran agreed to work towards a peace deal. Now, the incident happened as shipping traffic was just starting to pick up. Just days after the U.S. lifted sanctions on Iranian oil, allowing Tehran to sell oil in dollars for the first time in decades.
paragraphs.cn: 今晨最新消息，一艘船只在霍尔木兹海峡被击中。美国指责伊朗，称其无人机击中了这艘货船。这是自美国和伊朗同意努力达成和平协议以来的首次袭击。事件发生时，航运交通刚刚开始恢复。就在美国解除对伊朗石油制裁、允许德黑兰几十年来首次以美元出售石油几天后。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we're bringing in CNN's Paula Hancocks. Paula, as I said, consequential day, the lifting of sanctions. And then at the same time, you've got this strike occurring in the Strait of Hormuz. Can you talk about what that means for the ability of these negotiations to go on if there's still threats to the ships from passing through?
paragraphs.cn: 我们连线CNN的保拉·汉考克斯。保拉，正如我所说，今天是重要的一天，制裁解除。与此同时，霍尔木兹海峡发生了袭击。如果船只通过仍面临威胁，这对谈判的继续进行意味着什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So, Audie, it had been going so well in the Strait of Hormuz this week. With this latest strike, there were no casualties. There's no environmental damage. But the hit to confidence in the shipping industry is pretty significant. There was an evacuation that was underway by the U.N., by the international maritime organization to try and get the vessels and more than 11,000 seafarers who had been stuck in the Persian Gulf since the start of the war out into the open waters. Now that had begun.
paragraphs.cn: 奥蒂，本周霍尔木兹海峡的情况一直很顺利。这次最新袭击没有造成人员伤亡，也没有环境破坏。但对航运业信心的打击相当大。联合国和国际海事组织此前正在组织疏散，试图让自战争开始以来被困在波斯湾的船只和超过11000名海员进入公海。疏散已经开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: We saw on Wednesday some 70 vessels managed to transit through the Strait of Hormuz. But now that there has been this strike, that is all on hold. The U.N. saying they're halting this evacuation. They said the safety of the seafarers is the most important. They need more clarity. Now, Iran hasn't claimed responsibility at this point for this strike on the vessel, which the U.S. says was done by an Iranian drone.
paragraphs.cn: 周三我们看到大约70艘船只成功通过了霍尔木兹海峡。但现在发生了这次袭击，一切暂停。联合国表示他们正在停止这次疏散。他们说海员的安全是最重要的。他们需要更明确的信息。目前，伊朗尚未声称对这次袭击负责，美国称袭击是由伊朗无人机实施的。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: But what we heard just hours before this strike happened was the Revolutionary Guard in Iran saying that they couldn't guarantee safe passage for any vessel that wasn't using their route through the Strait of Hormuz. They want vessels to be going along the northern part of the Strait of Hormuz, so they have to go through Tehran, they have to fill in the forms that Tehran gives them, rather than the southern part, which is hugging the coast of Oman, which, understandably, most vessels are going through.
paragraphs.cn: 但就在这次袭击发生前几个小时，我们听到伊朗革命卫队表示，他们无法保证任何不通过他们指定的霍尔木兹海峡航线的船只的安全通行。他们希望船只沿着霍尔木兹海峡北部航行，这样就必须经过德黑兰，填写德黑兰提供的表格，而不是沿着南部靠近阿曼海岸的航线，而大多数船只显然都在走南部航线。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So, this really throws into doubt now the U.S. president's claim that the Strait of Hormuz is open and that it is free. And it really brings a halt to what we had seen earlier in this week with the Strait of Hormuz seeing a number of vessels coming through. The oil price dipping because of that. And, of course, gas prices as well dipping slightly. Now, what we've heard from the U.S. secretary of state, Marco Rubio, he's just been in this region.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，这确实让美国总统关于霍尔木兹海峡开放且自由的声明受到质疑。这也使得本周早些时候我们看到的多艘船只通过霍尔木兹海峡的情况戛然而止。油价因此下跌，当然，汽油价格也略有下降。现在，我们听到美国国务卿马可·鲁比奥刚刚访问了该地区。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: He was meeting some of the gulf nations who were really hardest hit by Iran in their retaliation during the war. And he has said that no country on earth should be able to claim responsibility over an international waterway. But that does appear to be exactly what Iran wants to do. It says that it's in talks with Oman, which is on the other side of the water. But Oman's foreign minister says that they support international law. They support a toll-free system.
paragraphs.cn: 他会见了一些海湾国家，这些国家在战争期间因伊朗的报复而遭受了最严重的打击。他表示，世界上任何国家都不应该声称对国际水道拥有管辖权。但这似乎正是伊朗想要做的。伊朗表示正在与水道另一侧的阿曼进行谈判。但阿曼外交部长表示，他们支持国际法，支持免费通行制度。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: So, at this point, Tehran is really out on its own when it is saying that it wants vessels to use its route. And of course, there is the issue of mines in the Strait of Hormuz as well. Presumably why no vessels are wanting to go through the center of this waterway. But it does throw into doubt the talks that have been ongoing.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，在这一点上，德黑兰声称希望船只使用其航线，实际上是孤立无援的。当然，还有霍尔木兹海峡的水雷问题。这大概就是为什么没有船只愿意通过这条水道的中心。但这确实给正在进行的谈判带来了不确定性。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: We heard from Secretary Rubio as well that that he's given a statement with the gulf nations here really widening the remit, also pointing out they have to deal with Iran's missiles and its use of proxies. Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 我们还听到鲁比奥国务卿与海湾国家发表声明，扩大了范围，并指出他们必须应对伊朗的导弹及其代理人的使用。奥蒂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's Paula Hancocks from Abu Dhabi. Thank you. I want to turn to this. The U.S. forces being sent to Venezuela as the nation deals with the aftermath of two devastating earthquakes. The death toll climbed to more than 230 people. It is still expected to rise. The country's health minister saying more than 4,300 people are injured. Rescue efforts ongoing, with hundreds of people trapped under rubble or missing. And Venezuelans are not only reeling from the physical destruction of their country.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，这是来自阿布扎比的保拉·汉考克斯。谢谢。我想转向另一个话题。美国军队被派往委内瑞拉，该国正在应对两场毁灭性地震的后果。死亡人数已升至230多人，预计还会上升。该国卫生部长表示，超过4300人受伤。救援工作正在进行，数百人被埋在废墟下或失踪。委内瑞拉人不仅因国家的物质破坏而震惊。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Critics say there's also a vacuum of authority in the country with the burden of rescue and relief efforts falling on civil organizations and schools and local churches and families. Joining me now, Mariana Atencio. She's a Venezuelan journalist, author and speaker. Thank you so much for being with us. I'm so sorry it's under these circumstances because I know you have friends and family still in Venezuela. What have you heard from them?
paragraphs.cn: 批评人士称，该国还存在权力真空，救援和救济工作的重担落在了民间组织、学校、当地教堂和家庭身上。现在请到的是玛丽安娜·阿滕西奥。她是委内瑞拉记者、作家和演讲者。非常感谢您来到我们的节目。很抱歉是在这种情况下，因为我知道您在委内瑞拉还有朋友和家人。您从他们那里听到了什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Mariana Atencio
paragraphs.en: Audie, these have been the most harrowing few days for Venezuelans. On the ground, local organizations estimate the number of missing could climb to 40,000 people. I'm seeing images of children that are left completely orphaned. And you stated it, there is a power vacuum in Venezuela right now. The worst hit areas of La Guaira. There's -- that's the coastal area. There's no heavy machinery. Neighbors are digging their own family members out of the rubble by hand. Hospitals are overwhelmed.
paragraphs.cn: 奥蒂，对委内瑞拉人来说，这是最痛苦的几天。在当地，当地组织估计失踪人数可能攀升至4万人。我看到一些孩子完全成了孤儿。正如你所说，委内瑞拉目前存在权力真空。受灾最严重的地区是拉瓜伊拉，那是沿海地区。那里没有重型机械。邻居们徒手从废墟中挖出自己的家人。医院不堪重负。

paragraphs.speaker: Mariana Atencio
paragraphs.en: They're treating the wounded in the streets because this earthquake is hitting a country that had nothing left to absorb it. And we're talking about a health system that's collapsed after years of crisis.
paragraphs.cn: 他们在街上治疗伤员，因为这场地震袭击了一个已经一无所有的国家。我们谈论的是一个经过多年危机已经崩溃的医疗系统。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You know, it's such a moment to be talking about this because here in the U.S. the Venezuelan asylum seekers who are already here, they've had their status terminated. We talked about that earlier, happening to Syrians and Haitians and others. But the U.S. now has a very peculiar relationship with Venezuela. Is there any sign of it now that the country is suffering?
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，现在谈论这个时机很特殊，因为在美国，已经在这里的委内瑞拉寻求庇护者的身份已被终止。我们之前讨论过，叙利亚人、海地人和其他人也遭遇了同样的情况。但美国现在与委内瑞拉的关系非常特殊。现在这个国家正在遭受苦难，有什么迹象表明关系会改变吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Atencio
paragraphs.en: So Rubio said the response will be big, fast, effective. President Trump called Venezuela our new and great friends. They've, of course, pledged $150 million in aid. But at the same time, I can speak for a diaspora of nearly eight million people, being sent back doesn't mean being sent back home right now. It means going back to rubble. So, it is ironic that the same week that Venezuela becomes a disaster zone, the U.S. is closing the door, TPS, for roughly 300,000 Venezuelans already terminated.
paragraphs.cn: 所以鲁比奥说，回应将是大规模、迅速且有效的。特朗普总统称委内瑞拉为我们新的伟大朋友。当然，他们承诺了1.5亿美元的援助。但与此同时，我可以代表近800万侨民说，被遣返并不意味着立即回到家乡。这意味着回到废墟中。因此，具有讽刺意味的是，就在委内瑞拉成为灾难区的同一周，美国却关上了大门，针对大约30万委内瑞拉人的临时保护身份已经终止。

paragraphs.speaker: Atencio
paragraphs.en: And that is what TPS is created for, Audie, temporary protected status. I mean, the Congressional Hispanic Caucus even made the same point in a letter to Rubio and Trump saying, the removals to a country in the midst of a disaster zone like this are untenable. And I'll bring up one more point in terms of the relationship with the U.S., which I think is critically important. And it's also this idea of, you are pledging all of this aid.
paragraphs.cn: 而这就是临时保护身份设立的目的，奥迪。我是说，国会拉美裔核心小组甚至在一封致鲁比奥和特朗普的信中提出了同样的观点，称将人员遣返到这样一个处于灾难中的国家是不可行的。我还要再提一点关于与美国的关系，我认为这一点至关重要。那就是你承诺了所有这些援助。

paragraphs.speaker: Atencio
paragraphs.en: This new regime, or new and great friends, need to be held accountable for how they administer that aid, but also for who they are locking up inside of Venezuela. There's around 500 to 600 innocent political prisoners that they are still holding behind bars. Those people, in the midst of this disaster, should be released to their families immediately.
paragraphs.cn: 这个新政权，或者说新的伟大朋友，需要对其如何管理援助负责，同时也要对他们在委内瑞拉境内关押了谁负责。目前仍有大约500到600名无辜的政治犯被关在监狱里。在这场灾难中，这些人应该立即被释放，回到家人身边。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Just one other thing. Delcy Rodriguez, we're talking about a vacuum. There is interim leadership. Leadership who had even been there under Maduro. Is the sense right now that they have an inability to or they are not capable of dealing with this moment?
paragraphs.cn: 还有一件事。德尔西·罗德里格斯，我们正在谈论一个真空。存在临时领导层。这些领导层甚至在马杜罗时期就已经存在。现在给人的感觉是他们无力或无法应对这一时刻吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Atencio
paragraphs.en: We can't really call this a government, Audie. This is -- this is a regime that has operated for years as a cartel that has been laundering money, stealing from Venezuelans, and has plundered our oil industry and our health system. So, this does not function like a regular government. This is a very big test for Delcy Rodriguez.
paragraphs.cn: 我们不能真的称这为一个政府，奥迪。这是一个多年来像卡特尔一样运作的政权，一直在洗钱、盗窃委内瑞拉人的财富，并掠夺了我们的石油工业和医疗系统。所以，它不像一个正常政府那样运作。这对德尔西·罗德里格斯来说是一个巨大的考验。

paragraphs.speaker: Atencio
paragraphs.en: But on the ground, what you are seeing are churches, our neighbors really just rising up to this moment, regular people, because the government has really an inability to respond to a crisis of this scale. So, it remains to be seen how they will mount a response and how they will administer the aid. But already I'm hearing very worrying reports of them saying that the aid should not be administered by opposition figures on the ground.
paragraphs.cn: 但在实地，你所看到的是教堂、我们的邻居真正挺身而出，普通民众，因为政府确实无力应对如此规模的危机。因此，他们将如何组织应对以及如何管理援助，还有待观察。但我已经听到非常令人担忧的报告，称他们表示援助不应由实地的反对派人物来管理。

paragraphs.speaker: Atencio
paragraphs.en: And this is a moment when Venezuela needs to come together as a nation to be able to overcome this incredible test.
paragraphs.cn: 而这是一个委内瑞拉需要团结一致、克服这一巨大考验的时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's journalist Mariana Atencio. Thank you. And if you are watching right now, and you want to know how you can help with the victims for the Venezuelan earthquake, I want you to go to cnn.com/impact. You can also just text the word "quake" to 707070. And next on CNN THIS MORNING, there's a new progressive voice rising. And Darializa Chevalier wins big in New York. Why some are saying she's the next AOC.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，那是记者玛丽安娜·阿滕西奥。谢谢。如果您正在观看，并想知道如何帮助委内瑞拉地震的受害者，请访问cnn.com/impact。您也可以将"quake"字样发送到707070。接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，将出现一位新的进步声音。达里亚利萨·舍瓦利耶在纽约大获全胜。为什么有人说她是下一个AOC。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Plus, Vice President Vance drawing criticism after saying the Watergate scandal would have been a blip in today's news cycle. And in the next hour on CNN NEWS CENTRAL, a Boeing 777 flies close to the ground in Texas. The FAA investigation now underway. REP. MIKE LAWLER (R-NY): I think it's a disaster for Democrats. I mean they took out very progressive Democratic members in favor of socialists.
paragraphs.cn: 此外，副总统万斯因称水门事件在今天的新闻周期中只会是一个小插曲而招致批评。下一小时在《CNN新闻中心》节目中，一架波音777在德克萨斯州低空飞行。美国联邦航空管理局的调查正在进行中。众议员迈克·劳勒（纽约州共和党人）：我认为这对民主党来说是一场灾难。我的意思是，他们淘汰了非常进步的民主党成员，转而支持社会主义者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Does the right have a new boogeyman? Democratic socialist Darializa Avila Chevalier, fresh off her big win Tuesday, now looking to the general. But DAC's comments about deportation, borders, prisons and past tweets are making her a target for not only the right but those in her party. One centrist Democrat put it like this, "if AOC was this Tea Party's Ted Cruz, yes, DAC is this Tea Party's MTG." I know it's a lot. "It's less substance and more about a sense that this person is unhinged and indefensible." REP.
paragraphs.cn: 右翼有了新的假想敌吗？民主社会主义者达里亚利萨·阿维拉·舍瓦利耶，在周二取得重大胜利后，现在正着眼于大选。但DAC关于驱逐出境、边境、监狱的言论以及过去的推文，使她不仅成为右翼的目标，也成为党内一些人的目标。一位中间派民主党人这样说道："如果AOC是茶党的特德·克鲁兹，那么是的，DAC就是茶党的MTG。"我知道这很复杂。"这更多是关于一种感觉，即这个人精神错乱且无可辩护，而非实质内容。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: ALEXANDRIA OCASIO-CORTE (D-NY): I feel like it's deja vu all over again. There was so much fear around that when I was elected. And none of it bore out to be true. Voters vote for what is happening in their community and what's on their ballot. And I think that any candidate of any party that's running their own race has the responsibility to run their race.
paragraphs.cn: 亚历山德里娅·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯（纽约州民主党）：我感觉这就像是历史重演。我当选时，周围充满了恐惧。但那些恐惧没有一件成真。选民投票是基于他们社区正在发生的事情以及选票上的内容。我认为，任何政党的候选人，只要是在竞选自己的职位，都有责任专注于自己的竞选。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this analogy because we're mentioning these names, Ted Cruz or AOC or MTG. Like, once you get into office, you have to deal with the realities of being in office and your positions change from when you are -- not your positions change, your tone and tenor can change depending. Lulu, what do you see in the way people have been talking about this particular candidate this week? Does it, like AOC says, feel familiar?
paragraphs.cn: 好了，群聊又回来了。我想谈谈这个类比，因为我们提到了这些名字：特德·克鲁兹、AOC或MTG。一旦你进入办公室，你就必须面对在任的现实，你的立场会发生变化 -- -- 不是立场变了，而是你的语气和基调会根据情况而改变。卢卢，这周人们谈论这位特定候选人的方式，你看到了什么？是否像AOC说的那样，感觉似曾相识？

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: It feels familiar. It wasn't a hot minute after she was elected that the right was just -- like you could -- they could not contain their joy. This is an absolute gift, in their view, to them. She has positions that are wildly out of the mainstream. I mean, let's be honest about this.
paragraphs.cn: 感觉似曾相识。她当选后不久，右翼就 -- -- 就像你 -- -- 他们简直无法掩饰自己的喜悦。在他们看来，这绝对是一份礼物。她的立场完全偏离主流。我的意思是，让我们对此坦诚一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And just to be clear. So, here's "The New York Post" tying her -- or tying just sort of Democratic socialists to Hochul, who's generally not a person I think of in that way. We're bringing in Marxism. We're going all the way. Socialism's not good enough.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且需要明确一点。所以，《纽约邮报》把她 -- -- 或者说把民主社会主义者与霍楚尔联系在一起，而霍楚尔通常不是我心目中那种人。我们引入了马克思主义。我们全力以赴。社会主义还不够好。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I mean they're basically saying that they're going to target this. I mean -- what --
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，他们基本上是在说他们要针对这一点。我的意思是 -- -- 什么 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But that makes sense too.
paragraphs.cn: 但这也有道理。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes, I mean the Democratic socialist project, and this is by their own admission, is that they do want a civil war with the Democratic Party. They think the Democratic Party has lost its way and they want to overtake it. And they think that it has not been forceful enough in pushing a very progressive agenda. That is different in some ways than making --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我的意思是，民主社会主义计划，而且他们自己也承认，他们确实想与民主党进行内战。他们认为民主党已经迷失了方向，他们想取代它。他们认为民主党在推动非常进步的议程方面不够有力。这在某些方面不同于 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Darializa the sort of poster child of this.
paragraphs.cn: 达里亚利扎是这方面的典型代表。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, then let me play one thing for you, because James Carville has always got to enter the chat.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，那我给你放一段，因为詹姆斯·卡维尔总是要加入讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Here he is, what he has to say
paragraphs.cn: 这是他说的。

paragraphs.speaker: James Carville
paragraphs.en: This Chevalier lady, I don't -- they should not seat her in the caucus. All of these people hate Democrats. Why do they want to run as a Democrat? Start your own movement. If it's such a powerful, sweeping movement that's got momentum everywhere, then go ahead and be at the head of it. Don't use the Democratic Party to advance it.
paragraphs.cn: 这位骑士女士，我不 -- -- 他们不应该让她参加党团会议。所有这些人都讨厌民主党人。他们为什么想以民主党人的身份参选？发起你自己的运动。如果这是一场如此强大、席卷一切、处处都有势头的运动，那就去领导它。不要利用民主党来推进它。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Rob, I got to ask you, because I've asked Republicans all week who may have deja vu from MAGA and Tea Party, does that work to just be like, y'all go somewhere else? I mean Donald Trump's president now. ROB BLUEY, PRESIDENT AND EXECUTIVE EDITOR, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Donald Trump is president. You have the House Freedom Caucus, which is a group that exerted its power, right? When you have these narrow margins.
paragraphs.cn: 罗布，我得问你，因为我整个星期都在问共和党人，他们可能对MAGA和茶党有似曾相识的感觉，那样说"你们都去别处吧"有用吗？我的意思是，唐纳德·特朗普现在是总统。罗布·布鲁伊，《每日信号》总裁兼执行主编：唐纳德·特朗普是总统。还有众议院自由党团，这是一个行使权力的团体，对吧？当你的优势很微弱的时候。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I mean, Hakeem Jeffries, right now, is probably looking at a big headache if he were to become speaker next year because he's going to have a large number of now Democratic socialists who would be able to hijack the floor and really set an agenda.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，哈基姆·杰弗里斯现在可能正面临一个大麻烦，如果他明年成为议长的话，因为他将拥有大量现在的民主党社会主义者，他们能够劫持会场并真正设定议程。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And if you look at that party's platform, not party, but I mean if you look at the Democratic socialist platform, I mean they have quite radical ideas, which I agree are out of step with not only independent and mainstream voters, but probably the Democratic Party as large.
paragraphs.cn: 如果你看看那个政党的纲领，不是政党，而是如果你看看民主社会主义者的纲领，我的意思是他们有相当激进的想法，我同意这些想法不仅与独立和主流选民脱节，而且可能与整个民主党脱节。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We'll at least, while you're talking, put up this "New York Times" polling piece. They were asked, should Democratic Party be -- move more to the center or to the left? And I have to admit, I wasn't surprised about the Republican thoughts on that. But the independent thoughts, 77 percent, at least of these people polled, saying more to the center. And even Democrats, it's 44 percent to the center.
paragraphs.cn: 至少，在你说话的时候，我们放上《纽约时报》的这篇民调文章。他们被问到，民主党应该 -- -- 更向中间还是向左移动？我不得不承认，我对共和党人的想法并不感到惊讶。但独立人士的想法，77%，至少在这些受访者中，说更向中间。甚至民主党人，44%说向中间。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And this is the split. The most enthusiastic and loudest in the primaries aren't maybe necessarily the people who are polled or who will be appearing in the general.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是分歧。初选中最热情、最响亮的人不一定就是那些被民调的人，或者会出现在大选中的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Yes. Nothing is shocking about those numbers here, partly because, of course, people in the center want a bigger center. It's a common American theme. Now, it's interesting that I think both of you had mentioned both the Tea Party and MAGA, and there are echoes of that here. There's a party in the wilderness. People -- there's discontent in the country. And folks from the outside just want to push their party in their direction. That's exactly what happened both times and led to Donald Trump to some extent.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这些数字一点也不令人震惊，部分原因是，当然，中间派想要一个更大的中间派。这是一个常见的美国主题。现在，有趣的是，我认为你们俩都提到了茶党和MAGA，这里有它们的影子。有一个在野的政党。人们 -- -- 这个国家存在不满。来自外部的人只是想把自己的政党推向他们的方向。这正是两次发生的事情，并在某种程度上导致了唐纳德·特朗普。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: You know, now, I -- does this lead to a Democratic socialist president? I think perhaps that's unlikely right now.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，现在，我 -- -- 这会导致一位民主社会主义者总统吗？我认为现在可能不太可能。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: We've seen these kinds of movements before.
paragraphs.cn: 我们以前见过这类运动。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I also just want to say that turnout was extremely low in these elections that brought these Democratic socialists out.
paragraphs.cn: 我还想说，在这些选出民主社会主义者的选举中，投票率极低。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: This was a very, very marginal election where very few people came out to vote. And the people who came out to vote were the most motivated young people who are very far left.
paragraphs.cn: 这是一场非常非常边缘的选举，投票的人很少。而出来投票的是那些最积极的年轻人，他们非常左倾。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. But I still care about it because lots of people look to New York for political playbooks. Things they do sometimes gets exported to other campaigns around the country.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。但我仍然关心它，因为很多人指望纽约提供政治策略。他们有时做的事情会被推广到全国其他竞选活动中。

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: And Mayor Mamdani --
paragraphs.cn: 而马姆达尼市长 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Wait one second. I'm still talking about 2028.
paragraphs.cn: 等一下。我还在说2028年的事。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.en: I want to come to this thing about J.D. Vance, because he's still been on tour with his book. He's making some claims now about Richard Nixon, the 37th president. The vice president was promoting at the New York presidential -- the Nixon Presidential Library in Yorba Linda, California. This was Thursday. And he was talking all about his admiration for Nixon, who, of course, is best known for resigning in disgrace.
paragraphs.cn: 我想谈谈J.D.万斯的事，因为他还在带着他的书巡回宣传。他现在对第37任总统理查德·尼克松发表了一些言论。这位副总统周四在加利福尼亚州约巴林达的尼克松总统图书馆进行了宣传。他大谈特谈对尼克松的钦佩，而尼克松当然以不光彩地辞职而闻名。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: If Watergate happened tomorrow, it would be like a 12-hour news story. Like, the idea that it would have taken down a presidency is crazy. And by the way, if you look at the story of how the deep state took down Richard Nixon, it's not all that different from what the same groups of people, the same institutions, tried to do to Donald Trump in the first Trump administration. There is a parallel.
paragraphs.cn: 如果水门事件发生在明天，那只会是一个12小时的新闻故事。认为它能搞垮一届总统的想法是疯狂的。顺便说一句，如果你看看深层政府搞垮理查德·尼克松的故事，就会发现这与同一群人、同一机构在特朗普第一届政府期间试图对唐纳德·特朗普做的事情没什么不同。两者有相似之处。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Group chat is back. There's a couple of Republicans, I mean maybe not still alive, but who might quibble with being called the deep state? Fundamentally, Nixon went down because of a bipartisan conversation about what happened, no?
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。有几个共和党人 -- -- 我的意思是可能还活着 -- -- 但可能会对被称作深层政府有异议？从根本上说，尼克松倒台是因为两党对发生的事情进行了讨论，不是吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Sure. Well, I -- yes. So, I say a couple of things on this. Number one, let's remember historically that Richard Nixon greatly expanded government. And conservatives were no fan of Richard Nixon for the creation of the EPA and other government agencies. So, I think we do need to put that in the historical context.
paragraphs.cn: 当然。嗯，我 -- -- 是的。所以，我对此说几点。第一，让我们从历史角度记住，理查德·尼克松极大地扩张了政府。保守派并不喜欢理查德·尼克松，因为他创建了环保署和其他政府机构。所以，我认为我们需要将其放在历史背景中看待。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But this one is. Vance has comment -- has mimicked Nixon in talking about certain ideas.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但这一点是。万斯在谈论某些想法时模仿了尼克松。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And so, who are these (INAUDIBLE) here?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，这些（听不清）是谁？

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Nixon is making a resurgence.
paragraphs.cn: 尼克松正在卷土重来。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Like, I'm so sorry we're trying to bring back Nixon now?
paragraphs.cn: 比如，我很抱歉我们现在要重新推崇尼克松吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Well, but Bill Clinton did as well. I mean he's --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，但比尔·克林顿也这样做了。我的意思是，他 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I mean, do we have to like -- do we have to -- do we have to revisit what he actually did? Do you -- I mean I feel like reading David Axelrod street (ph). Just to review, he authorized the break-in of the DNC to install bugging equipment. He then enlisted the CIA to mislead the FBI. Then the entire operation was paid for by a slush fund controlled by the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我们有必要 -- -- 我们有必要 -- -- 我们有必要重新审视他实际做了什么吗？你 -- -- 我觉得就像在读大卫·阿克塞尔罗德的书。简单回顾一下，他授权闯入民主党全国委员会安装窃听设备。然后他利用中央情报局误导联邦调查局。整个行动由白宫控制的秘密资金支付。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: However, we are now Nixon maxing. And if you check out the Nixon Foundation Instagram page, which is very hip these days.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，我们现在正在最大化尼克松。如果你去看看尼克松基金会的Instagram页面，现在非常时髦。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There is a lot of capital c content kind of rewriting his story. And actually, one of the top popular comments from one of the viewers is, Watergate was a lie, you know what I mean, with a bunch of heart, likes and --
paragraphs.cn: 有很多内容在重写他的故事。实际上，观众中一个最热门的评论是，水门事件是个谎言，你懂我的意思，还带着一堆爱心、点赞和 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: With the heart eyes thing.
paragraphs.cn: 还有那个爱心眼的表情。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Yes, I just don't know if celebrating Richard Nixon is the burn that folks think it is. And just to the points that Lulu was making before, simply on account of his universally accepted misdeeds, he ultimately had to leave the presidency because his party turned on him, regardless of, you know, the merits of anything (ph). Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我只是不知道庆祝理查德·尼克松是否像人们想的那样是一种反击。正如露露之前指出的，仅仅因为他普遍公认的不当行为，他最终不得不离开总统职位，因为他的政党抛弃了他，不管其他方面如何。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, to that point, Vance said something that I think is absolutely true, which is, in this day and age, that would be a blip in the news cycle.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，关于这一点，万斯说了一些我认为绝对正确的话，那就是，在当今时代，那只会是新闻周期中的一个小插曲。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: If you think of the kinds of things we have been reporting on for the last six, eight, ten years, the break in is looking smaller and smaller compared to a --
paragraphs.cn: 如果你想想我们在过去六、八、十年里报道的那些事情，闯入事件相比之下显得越来越微不足道 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: What does that say about our current political moment. I mean this is -- my bigger problem with what J.D. Vance actually said is that it actually reflects quite poorly, perhaps, on his administration.
paragraphs.cn: 这说明了我们当前的政治时刻什么？我的意思是，我对J.D.万斯实际所说的话更大的不满在于，它实际上可能相当负面地反映了他的政府。

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Well, I think you have to go back even further. I mean Bill Clinton having sex in the Oval Office. Your own colleague, Jake Tapper, reporting about the cover-up of Joe Biden's mental acuity. I mean, so it's not just Donald Trump. I think we need to broaden the --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为你得追溯到更早。我是说比尔·克林顿在椭圆形办公室发生性行为。你自己的同事杰克·塔珀报道了掩盖乔·拜登心智敏锐度的问题。我的意思是，这不只是唐纳德·特朗普的问题。我认为我们需要扩大 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, you would agree. The -- it wouldn't have been the crisis it is now, mainly because we have crazy things going on.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以你会同意。这 -- -- 它不会成为现在这样的危机，主要是因为我们在经历疯狂的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You can name all the presidents you want. That's great.
paragraphs.cn: 你可以列举所有你想要的总统。那很好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All comers accepted. Also and it feels as though the moral sort of outcry that marked the Watergate era, that era of politics is done.
paragraphs.cn: 所有挑战都接受。而且感觉好像标志着水门事件时代的道德谴责，那个政治时代已经结束了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The Supreme Court's like, don't worry about it. The executive can be more powerful. The Congress are like, you want to do --
paragraphs.cn: 最高法院就像，别担心。行政分支可以更强大。国会就像，你想做 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Trump has gotten billions of dollars while in office. So has his family.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普在任期间获得了数十亿美元。他的家人也是如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I haven't seen an oversight hearing in weeks.
paragraphs.cn: 我已经好几个星期没看到监督听证会了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's just one of those things where Washington itself doesn't behave this way anymore.
paragraphs.cn: 这只是华盛顿本身不再这样行事的事情之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: I think few people are going to defend Bill Clinton and his conduct. I think we can universally agree with that. You know, Richard Nixon --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为很少有人会为比尔·克林顿及其行为辩护。我想我们可以普遍同意这一点。你知道，理查德·尼克松 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: At least there were hearings.
paragraphs.cn: 至少还有听证会。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Richard Nixon had a documented history of anti-Semitic comments in sort of -- to people that he thought were trusted. He spied on his allies. He cheapened the American political system. Regardless of anything he did, you know, as a successful politician, we ought not be celebrating Richard Nixon here.
paragraphs.cn: 理查德·尼克松有记录显示他曾对他认为值得信任的人发表反犹太言论。他监视自己的盟友。他贬低了美国的政治体制。不管他作为成功的政治家做了什么，我们都不应该在这里庆祝理查德·尼克松。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Elliot, not Nixon maxing. Got it.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，埃利奥特，不是尼克松最大化。明白了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Not in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: 不在你的群聊里。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: But I will say this, look, that the '70s with big hair is back. We got a World Cup. Gas is expensive. Nixon is back. Let's go back to '74.
paragraphs.cn: 但我要说的是，看，70年代的大发型又回来了。我们有世界杯。油价很贵。尼克松回来了。让我们回到74年。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. I'm sure the administration is glad it's not Carter.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我相信政府很高兴不是卡特。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Glad it's not Carter (ph).
paragraphs.cn: 很高兴不是卡特。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Rob, you haven't been here in a while. What's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: 罗布，你有一阵子没来了。你的群聊里有什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Sure. Well, the Great American State Fair is taking place on the National Mall. And the U.S. State Department has just announced that they're giving a limited number of U.S. passports to celebrate America 250. And if you want one, it has Donald Trump's picture inside.
paragraphs.cn: 当然。嗯，伟大的美国州博览会在国家广场举行。美国国务院刚刚宣布，他们将发放有限数量的美国护照来庆祝美国250周年。如果你想要一本，里面会有唐纳德·特朗普的照片。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. And you -- and for you?
paragraphs.cn: 好的。那你呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: McDonalds has brought back the apple -- the fried apple pie.
paragraphs.cn: 麦当劳重新推出了苹果 -- -- 油炸苹果派。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, man, that is American.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，天哪，这很美国。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Because, you know what? And, you know -- and get -- it is American. You know what, back to the '70s being back. I feel like, as a child of the '70s, every kid needs to celebrate burning the hell out of your mouth on that cardboard, too sweet, overly spicy apple pie. It's when you --
paragraphs.cn: 因为，你知道吗？而且，你知道 -- -- 这很美国。你知道吗，回到70年代的感觉。作为70年代的孩子，我觉得每个孩子都需要庆祝一下，把嘴烫得够呛，吃那种太甜、香料味过重的纸板苹果派。当你 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You mean the delicate pastry that --
paragraphs.cn: 你是说那种精致的糕点 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And I am thrilled that it's back. I'm going to leave here --
paragraphs.cn: 我很高兴它回来了。我离开这里后 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Bluey
paragraphs.en: Sounds like we're going to --
paragraphs.cn: 听起来我们要 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And go shovel 15 in my mouth.
paragraphs.cn: 然后往嘴里塞15个。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Yes. No, no, quick, to bring it all back, it's actually fried, not baked now, but (INAUDIBLE) now.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。不，不，快，为了让它完全回归，现在是油炸的，不是烤的，但（听不清）现在。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Brought to you by McDonald's. Thank you guys in the group chat. Thank you for waking up with us. I'm Audie Cornish. Headlines now. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 好的。由麦当劳为您带来。谢谢群聊里的各位。谢谢你们和我们一起醒来。我是奥蒂·科尼什。现在播报新闻。

vocabulary.en: to examine something carefully, especially in order to find something
vocabulary.excerpt: first responders sift through the rubble
vocabulary.exampleCn: 急救人员在废墟中仔细搜寻。

vocabulary.cn: 黄金窗口期（救援关键期）
vocabulary.en: the critical period (usually 48-72 hours) during which rescue efforts are most likely to save lives
vocabulary.excerpt: The first 48 to 72 hours are widely regarded as the golden window to reach people
vocabulary.exampleCn: 最初的48到72小时被广泛认为是救援的黄金窗口期。

vocabulary.en: the area of an airport where planes are parked and loaded
vocabulary.excerpt: the powerful tremors shook the aircraft on the tarmac
vocabulary.exampleCn: 强烈的震动使停机坪上的飞机摇晃。

vocabulary.cn: （建筑）像煎饼一样坍塌叠压的
vocabulary.en: collapsed in a flat, layered manner, like a stack of pancakes
vocabulary.excerpt: all of these floors that have been pancaked on top of each other
vocabulary.exampleCn: 所有那些像煎饼一样层层叠压的楼板。

vocabulary.en: a large machine used for digging
vocabulary.excerpt: what they need in La Guaira is excavator; big machinery
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们在拉瓜伊拉需要的是挖掘机，大型机械。

vocabulary.en: a person who has fled their home country and is asking for protection in another country
vocabulary.excerpt: immigration officials at the U.S.-Mexico border can turn away asylum seekers before they enter the country
vocabulary.exampleCn: 移民官员可以在美墨边境的寻求庇护者进入美国之前将其拒之门外。

vocabulary.en: a statement by a judge who disagrees with the majority decision
vocabulary.excerpt: in her dissent, Justice Sonia Sotomayor wrote
vocabulary.exampleCn: 在她的异议意见中，索尼娅·索托马约尔大法官写道。

vocabulary.cn: 临时保护身份（美国移民政策）
vocabulary.en: a temporary immigration status granted to nationals of certain countries experiencing conflict or disaster
vocabulary.excerpt: the Trump administration to strip temporary protected status for hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians
vocabulary.exampleCn: 特朗普政府取消数十万海地人和叙利亚人的临时保护身份。

vocabulary.en: to remove something that is officially given
vocabulary.excerpt: the Trump administration to strip temporary protected status
vocabulary.exampleCn: 特朗普政府剥夺临时保护身份。

vocabulary.en: a written law passed by a legislative body
vocabulary.excerpt: The whole statute exists. Temporarily to give people protection
vocabulary.exampleCn: 整个法令的存在是为了临时给予人们保护。

vocabulary.en: to have the courage to do something
vocabulary.excerpt: no administration has had the guts to actually follow that statute
vocabulary.exampleCn: 没有哪届政府有勇气真正遵守那项法令。

vocabulary.en: a status that is partly but not fully legal
vocabulary.excerpt: this law from the 1990s gives people a kind of quasi legal status
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这项1990年代的法律赋予人们一种准合法身份。

vocabulary.word: with the stroke of a pen
vocabulary.en: by simply signing a document; easily and quickly
vocabulary.excerpt: It was just simply, with the stroke of a pen, we're go
vocabulary.exampleCn: 只是大笔一挥，我们就……

vocabulary.en: a punishment of being killed; something that causes certain death
vocabulary.excerpt: they have to go back there anyway, even to their death sentence
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们无论如何都得回去，哪怕是去送死。

vocabulary.en: a theory that a hidden network of government officials manipulates policy
vocabulary.excerpt: Vice President J.D. Vance compares himself to Richard Nixon and says Watergate was a deep-state conspiracy
vocabulary.exampleCn: 副总统J.D.万斯将自己比作理查德·尼克松，并称水门事件是深层政府的阴谋。

vocabulary.en: very surprised or shocked; defeated unexpectedly
vocabulary.excerpt: Team USA, stunned by Turkey in the World Cup
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国队在世界杯上被土耳其队击败，令人震惊。

vocabulary.en: to get rid of something unpleasant
vocabulary.excerpt: Can they shake off the bad vibes and bounce back?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们能摆脱负面情绪并反弹吗？

vocabulary.en: to recover quickly from a setback
vocabulary.excerpt: Can they shake off the bad vibes and bounce back?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们能摆脱负面情绪并反弹吗？

vocabulary.en: the number of people killed in a disaster, war, etc.
vocabulary.excerpt: The earthquake death toll continues to rise
vocabulary.exampleCn: 地震死亡人数持续上升。

vocabulary.en: emergency workers who arrive first at the scene of an accident or disaster
vocabulary.excerpt: first responders sift through the rubble
vocabulary.exampleCn: 急救人员在废墟中搜寻。

vocabulary.en: the area where relief operations are concentrated
vocabulary.excerpt: arrive as quickly and as close as possible to the relief zone
vocabulary.exampleCn: 尽可能快速、靠近地抵达救援区。

vocabulary.cn: 罢免，驱逐（尤指通过政变）
vocabulary.en: the act of removing someone from a position of power
vocabulary.excerpt: less than six months after the ousting of Maduro
vocabulary.exampleCn: 在马杜罗被罢免后不到六个月。

vocabulary.en: a military transport aircraft manufactured by Lockheed Martin
vocabulary.excerpt: The Colombians were also able to fly into Caracas with two Hercules C-130
vocabulary.exampleCn: 哥伦比亚人也能够用两架C-130大力神运输机飞入加拉加斯。

vocabulary.en: a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces responsible for amphibious operations
vocabulary.excerpt: A major general from the Marine Corps arrived in Caracas
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一位来自海军陆战队的少将抵达加拉加斯。

sentences.en: The earthquake death toll continues to rise.
sentences.cn: 地震死亡人数持续上升。
sentences.structure: 简单主谓结构：主语（The earthquake death toll）+ 谓语（continues to rise）。
sentences.analysis: 新闻常用表达，'death toll' 意为死亡人数，'continues to rise' 表示持续上升，体现事态发展。

sentences.en: The first 48 to 72 hours are widely regarded as the golden window to reach people who may still be buried alive.
sentences.cn: 最初的48到72小时被广泛视为接触可能仍被活埋人员的黄金窗口期。
sentences.structure: 主句（The first 48 to 72 hours are widely regarded as the golden window）+ 不定式短语（to reach people）+ 定语从句（who may still be buried alive）。
sentences.analysis: 'golden window' 是救援术语，指最佳救援时间；'buried alive' 意为被活埋；被动语态 'are widely regarded as' 体现普遍观点。

sentences.en: This is new video showing passengers panicking aboard a plane at the international airport North of Caracas as the powerful tremors shook the aircraft on the tarmac.
sentences.cn: 这是一段新视频，显示在加拉加斯以北的国际机场，当强烈震动摇晃停机坪上的飞机时，乘客在机上惊慌失措。
sentences.structure: 主句（This is new video）+ 现在分词短语（showing passengers panicking）+ 地点状语（at the international airport North of Caracas）+ 时间状语从句（as the powerful tremors shook the aircraft on the tarmac）。
sentences.analysis: 'showing' 引导伴随动作；'as' 引导时间状语从句，表示同时发生；'panicking' 描述乘客状态；'tarmac' 指停机坪。

sentences.en: I think that that is a smart point, a smart angle to explore today, is that today is the day we are finally seeing the aid coming in.
sentences.cn: 我认为这是一个聪明的观点，一个今天值得探讨的聪明角度，那就是今天我们终于看到援助到来。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（that that is a smart point, a smart angle to explore today）+ 表语从句（is that today is the day）+ 定语从句（we are finally seeing the aid coming in）。
sentences.analysis: 多个 'that' 的用法：第一个 'that' 引导宾语从句，第二个 'that' 作主语；'the day' 后省略关系副词 'when'；'seeing the aid coming in' 是感官动词结构。

sentences.en: The Colombians were also able to fly into Caracas with two Hercules C-130, which, of course, took time, because the runway itself at the international airport, which serves the city of Caracas, was badly damaged.
sentences.cn: 哥伦比亚人也能够用两架C-130大力神运输机飞入加拉加斯，这当然需要时间，因为服务于加拉加斯市的国际机场跑道本身严重受损。
sentences.structure: 主句（The Colombians were also able to fly into Caracas with two Hercules C-130）+ 非限制性定语从句（which, of course, took time）+ 原因状语从句（because the runway itself at the international airport, which serves the city of Caracas, was badly damaged），其中包含另一个非限制性定语从句（which serves the city of Caracas）。
sentences.analysis: 嵌套定语从句：第一个 'which' 指代整个主句，第二个 'which' 修饰 'the international airport'；'of course' 为插入语；'Hercules C-130' 是军用运输机型号。

sentences.en: You can see from that video that you just played that the quake hit with the plane on the tarmac and created open cracks, really, on the tarmac of the runway.
sentences.cn: 你可以从你刚才播放的视频中看到，地震发生时飞机正在停机坪上，并在跑道停机坪上造成了明显的裂缝。
sentences.structure: 主句（You can see from that video）+ 定语从句（that you just played）+ 宾语从句（that the quake hit with the plane on the tarmac and created open cracks, really, on the tarmac of the runway）。
sentences.analysis: 'see from...that' 结构；'with the plane on the tarmac' 是独立主格结构，表示伴随状态；'really' 为插入语，加强语气；'open cracks' 指明显的裂缝。

sentences.en: So, these military planes that carry the aid had to make some adjustments in order to arrive as quickly and as close as possible to the relief zone, which is the state of La Guaira, where the airport is located, which is this coastal state close to Caracas, but on the coast on the Caribbean coast, where the majority of the damage is -- and being confined.
sentences.cn: 因此，这些运送援助的军用飞机不得不做出一些调整，以便尽可能快速、尽可能接近地抵达救援区，即拉瓜伊拉州，机场所在地位于那里，这是一个靠近加拉加斯的沿海州，但在加勒比海岸线上，大部分损失都集中在那里。
sentences.structure: 主句（these military planes had to make some adjustments）+ 定语从句（that carry the aid）+ 目的状语（in order to arrive as quickly and as close as possible to the relief zone）+ 非限制性定语从句（which is the state of La Guaira）+ 非限制性定语从句（where the airport is located）+ 同位语（which is this coastal state close to Caracas, but on the coast on the Caribbean coast）+ 定语从句（where the majority of the damage is -- and being confined）。
sentences.analysis: 多个定语从句层层修饰；'as quickly and as close as possible' 是并列比较结构；'relief zone' 指救援区；'confined' 意为集中、局限。

sentences.en: By the way, it's still surreal to me that we are seeing a major marine general in the grounds of Caracas less than six months after the ousting of Maduro.
sentences.cn: 顺便说一句，在马杜罗被推翻不到六个月后，我们在加拉加斯看到一位海军陆战队少将，这对我来说仍然超现实。
sentences.structure: 主句（it's still surreal to me）+ 主语从句（that we are seeing a major marine general in the grounds of Caracas less than six months after the ousting of Maduro）。
sentences.analysis: 'it' 作形式主语，真正主语是 'that' 从句；'surreal' 意为超现实的；'ousting' 指推翻、罢免；'major marine general' 是海军陆战队少将。

sentences.en: I think heavy machinery is the trickiest thing, because one thing is to bring in containers with medicines, with plasters, with emergency kit, with generators.
sentences.cn: 我认为重型机械是最棘手的事情，因为带进装有药品、石膏、急救包、发电机的集装箱是一回事。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think heavy machinery is the trickiest thing）+ 原因状语从句（because one thing is to bring in containers with medicines, with plasters, with emergency kit, with generators）。
sentences.analysis: 'trickiest' 意为最棘手的；'one thing is to...' 结构暗示对比，后文应有 'another thing is...'；多个 'with' 并列列举物品。

sentences.en: But what do they need in La Guaira is excavator; big machinery in order to lift in a safely way, the -- all of these floors that have been pancaked on top of each other because of the -- of the power of the tremors.
sentences.cn: 但他们在拉瓜伊拉需要的是挖掘机；大型机械，以便安全地抬起那些因地震威力而层层叠压的楼板。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what do they need in La Guaira）+ 系表结构（is excavator）+ 同位语（big machinery）+ 目的状语（in order to lift in a safely way）+ 宾语（the -- all of these floors）+ 定语从句（that have been pancaked on top of each other）+ 原因状语（because of the -- of the power of the tremors）。
sentences.analysis: 'what' 引导主语从句；'pancaked' 是新闻常用词，形容建筑物像煎饼一样层层坍塌；'in a safely way' 应为 'in a safe way'，可能是口误；破折号表示犹豫或修正。

sentences.en: Our team is at the city, and they have been filming it.
sentences.cn: 我们的团队在该市，他们一直在拍摄。
sentences.structure: 并列句：主语（Our team）+ 系表（is at the city）+ 并列连词（and）+ 主语（they）+ 现在完成进行时（have been filming it）。
sentences.analysis: 简单并列句，但 'have been filming' 强调动作持续进行。

sentences.en: But in La Guaira yesterday, when they were down in the area most affected, they were really talking about the lack of equipment, the lack of gear.
sentences.cn: 但昨天在拉瓜伊拉，当他们到达受灾最严重的地区时，他们确实在谈论缺乏设备和装备的问题。
sentences.structure: 时间状语（yesterday）+ 地点状语（in La Guaira）+ 时间状语从句（when they were down in the area most affected）+ 主句（they were really talking about the lack of equipment, the lack of gear）。
sentences.analysis: 'down in' 表示深入某地；'most affected' 是过去分词短语作定语，修饰 'area'；'lack of equipment, the lack of gear' 并列强调短缺。

sentences.en: Now, while the people in Venezuela are desperate for help, we here in the U.S. have seen two rulings from the Supreme Court indicating that the U.S. is not quite the place of refuge it might have been.
sentences.cn: 现在，当委内瑞拉人民急需帮助时，我们美国这里却看到了最高法院的两项裁决，表明美国已不再是曾经的那个避难所。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句（while the people in Venezuela are desperate for help）+ 主句（we here in the U.S. have seen two rulings from the Supreme Court）+ 现在分词短语（indicating that the U.S. is not quite the place of refuge it might have been），其中包含宾语从句（that the U.S. is not quite the place of refuge）+ 定语从句（it might have been）。
